user_query:
You are acting as our outsourced Data Privacy lead. Please read the following inputs under input/:
- input/company_profile.json for our company context and jurisdictions
- input/processing_seed.json for preliminary processing activities and data flows
- input/vendors.csv for our current processors/sub-processors
- input/breach_scenario.txt for a recent incident that needs to be captured

Based on these inputs, produce the following deliverables under output/ using the templates and best practices you recommend:
1) output/applicability.json — A concise, structured matrix of which regulations apply to us (at minimum evaluate GDPR, UK GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and LGPD) with a short trigger explanation per regulation.
2) output/ropa.json — Our Record of Processing Activities as an array of processing_activity objects (≥4). Each entry must include: id, name, description, controller, dpo_contact, purpose, lawful_basis, data_subjects, data_categories (with sensitivity), source, storage_location, recipients (internal/processors/third_parties/cross_border), retention_period, retention_justification, deletion_method, security_measures, dpia_required, owner, last_reviewed, next_review, status. At least one activity should involve special category or high-sensitivity data and explicitly document the additional condition, and at least one activity must have dpia_required set to true.
3) output/dpia.json — A Data Protection Impact Assessment for the highest-risk processing from the seed file (the AI/profiling use case, if present). Include id, project, date, assessor, description (nature/scope/context/purpose/lawful_basis), necessity and proportionality, a minimum of 3 distinct risks with likelihood, severity, and risk_score, and at least 3 mitigations tied to those risks. Include a decision with residual_risk_acceptable and supervisory_authority_consultation.
4) output/dsar_workflow.yaml — An operational DSAR workflow aligned to our footprint, with explicit response timelines for GDPR (30 days) and CCPA/CPRA (45 days), verification steps, processor notification, and a minimal metrics block. Use clear keys so that a script can read gdpr_response_days and ccpa_response_days.
5) output/vendor_assessments.json — Vendor/processor assessments for at least 2 vendors from input/vendors.csv, each as an object with vendor, service, data_types, assessment_date, scores (security_posture, data_handling, contractual_terms, breach_history, sub_processor_mgmt, cross_border, reputation, total), decision ("Approve" | "Approve with conditions" | "Reject"), conditions (if any), and review_frequency.
6) output/breach_register.json — A breach register entry derived from input/breach_scenario.txt with: id, date_detected, date_contained, nature, cause, data_subjects_affected (estimate), records_affected (estimate), data_categories, risk_level, authority_notified (boolean), subjects_notified (boolean), root_cause, remediation, lessons_learned.
7) output/breach_playbook.md — A succinct breach response playbook referencing 72-hour authority notification where applicable and the phases from detection to recovery.

Make sure all files are valid JSON/YAML/MD, use plain English, are specific to our scenario, and reflect cross-border transfer mechanisms where relevant. If you make reasonable assumptions, document them briefly at the top of each file as comments (YAML) or a short "Assumptions" section (JSON/MD).
